candidates.author: ih-codes

candidates.title: fix #488 - support user field for completion
candidates.body:
- a few other backends use `user` instead of `username` or `login`
  (e.g. gopass-jsonapi, keepass2csv importer, fpm2 importer)
- shorter to type when extracting field separately (e.g. CLI/clipboard) tools

fixes #488
issue_titles: Add support for user: as well as username: for gopass repos
issue_bodies:
passforios looks for the string "username" when displaying login names and filling in forms in iOS.

The gopass-jsonapi writes "user" when writing to gopass

https://github.com/gopasspw/gopass-jsonapi/blob/main/internal/jsonapi/responses.go#L196

Can we get passforios to look for the strings "username", "login" and "user" to cover more backends?
candidates.author: MartinNowak

candidates.title: Add handoff support for code blob views
candidates.body:
I'm using handoff quite extensively (it's a great feature for me). Thus I did code blob view handoff just because sometimes it's easier to read code in a bigger screen.

There were no feature requests and from #331 I see that it's not expected that people will use handoff much. So if it looks like not a useful feature feel free to close it.
issue_bodies:
Ref #328.

While implementing this I was wondering though... opening a web page with Handoff would automatically mark an issue as read (as you would load the page on GitHub.com). Do we maybe want to only allow Handoff when the user has this setting enabled?

This would only make sense for the issues though - we can still support handoff for readmes, profiles etc. Not sure what would make most sense here.
candidates.author: viktorasl

candidates.title: Support inbox zero fixed holidays
candidates.body:
Closes #2560
Found some issues w/ language & region (https://github.com/GitHawkApp/Holidays/issues/3)
issue_bodies:
I noticed that my inbox doesn’t have the expected 🎅 icon today. It looks like the code that sees if there’s a special icon looks first for a mapping for this year, then for a fixed mapping. Instead, it should try to get the **day** from each key. Example code: ```swift let today = data["\(year)"]?["\(month)"]?["\(day)"] ?? data["fixed"]?["\(month)"]?["\(day)"] ``` Merry Christmas!
Bug Report Dump (Auto-generated) 
Version 1.26.0 (1544968988)
Device: iPhone 8 (iOS 12.1)
TestFlight: true
candidates.author: viktorasl

candidates.title: fix: update the current date when changing tabs
candidates.body:
* Fix #325
* Previously the date was only update once the app start. So at some
point if the app survive a night, the date will be wrong. To prevent
that, we update now the date every time the tab is changed
issue_bodies:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2297556/95052311-007bdd00-06ef-11eb-8675-3f2e87ec7298.png)
As you can see, the date of Today is not updated if the app was already open from the day before (the date is still the 4th October while today is the 5th).

We can either update the date when the app goes to foreground, or adding a pull to refresh on the first tab. Or both 😳. 😂.
candidates.author: renaudjenny

candidates.title: (Fix) autofill / extensions and restore persistent storage location
candidates.body:
Fixes: #685 
Fixes: #684 
Fixes: #683

There are 2 changes here to restore autofill / extension functionality

##     Add persistent DB path for extension access
    
Using the existing sqlite DB path maintains backwards compatibility with
the existing persistent store.

Assuming the DB path should be maintained from v0.16

This should fix issues reported where there are no entries for the
repository.

##     Move persistence controller setup into the init
    
App extensions access the persistent storage. From the refactor, the
persistent storage "setup" is no longer called which breaks the
extension and the pass auto fill.

This commit moves the persistent storage setup into the init for the
shared storage, which should be called once for the first read access or
in preparation for a write.

As a result, extensions should now work as expected.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/71354398-7f8f-4126-9cd7-996d10f7c021
issue_titles: Possible regression in 0.17 - empty passwords list
Cannot copy password / cannot decrypt from safari
issue_bodies:
I am not sure, but I guess that this commit:

https://github.com/mssun/passforios/commit/d1de81d919d5339913ec70055ff50c1ac93bd54d#diff-84b24e0597b72ae8f6025757de61d71b6b78d3c731bfd80ea0c2a8c57ed48007L27

might have caused a regression.

Earlier (checked in v0.16 using TestFlight), from Safari/other apps autocompletion works as expected.
Example:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f5564ee-bcd7-4351-b147-9245e9e94224)

In 0.17, I have empty list:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0ac0e93b-bfd5-43e7-a362-abe7970c87ab)

Even if I manually search by: l, li, lichess, lichess.org - nothing is working.
At the same time, the search within the app works fine. Seems like it's issue with browser suggestions only.
The repo has disappeared for the IOS device.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a9f812c-96d3-4e0a-bbd1-2fb0749cdb6d)

This is on the latest version on the App Store (v0.17.0), previously working / nothing changed. Password is viewable and copy able from within the pass app but no longer able to copy password via safari integration
candidates.author: a-feld

candidates.title: Use a smaller fragment when you fetch the backer attached to a Backing
candidates.body:
# 📲 What

This uses `PublicUserFragment` when you fetch the `backer` associated with a `Backing`.

(This review follows review #2748)

# 🤔 Why

1. I didn't run any exacting profiling, but cleaning this up leads to a perceivable improvement in the performance of the Profile page on my account. We were recursively fetching your Backings, effectively by querying `Backing { backer { backings } }` when `UserFragment` calling `backings`.
2. `UserFragment` queries a lot of private fields. While in many cases, this is  correct - because you're usually looking at your own backings - it's not always true. If you're a creator messaging a backer, we use `BackingFragment` to fetch the _backer's_ backing, and then you should only be able to see public fields.


# 🤨  Why now?

I'm working on some refactoring to the Project page, focusing on improving its speed (both perceived, and actual). This GraphQL cleanup is an important simplification step as I dig into the guts of how the project page loads.

Creating this more limited fragment makes the code clearer, and eliminates a bunch of unnecessary variable passing in GraphQL.
issue_titles: Use a smaller fragment when you fetch the creator attached to a Project
issue_bodies:
# 📲 What

This creates a new fragment, `PublicUserFragment`, which is used by `ProjectFragment` to fetch the project's creator.

# 🤔 Why

`ProjectFragment` was always fetching a full `UserFragment`, but `UserFragment` is really designed to be used when fetching the _current_ user. It includes a lot of private information, like newsletter subscriptions, the user's stored cards, and so on. None of this was relevant if you're just grabbing information about who made a project, unless it happened to be yours. The backend was returning `null` for all of those fields.

Creating this more limited fragment makes the code clearer, and eliminates a bunch of unnecessary variable passing in GraphQL.

# 🤨  Why now?

I'm working on some refactoring to the Project page, focusing on improving its speed (both perceived, and actual). This GraphQL cleanup is an important simplification step as I dig into the guts of how the project page loads.
candidates.author: amy-at-kickstarter

candidates.title: Move to Swift Package Manager disregarding Cocoapods
candidates.body:
Now that SPM is integrated into Xcode and works for iOS project, many project seem to move to it. I would like to give it a try, too. The project setup becomes a bit easier and the project structure looks clearer, I think.

* Build as many dependencies as possible with SPM.
* Xcworkspace is no longer needed.
* Collecting coverage information does unfortunately cause linking errors in Base32. So it is disabled for now.
* Build XCFrameworks with Carthage.

The build of the ObjectiveGit fork fails with a linker error. This has nothing to do with the switch to SPM, though. I'm not sure why the fork is needed at all. There was some activity in the upstream repository recently, so maybe we can move back to it? Turns out that this would also fix #490. Otherwise I can replace `"libgit2/objective-git"` by `"mssun/objective-git"` again.
issue_titles: SSH clone fails with "Unsupported URL protocol" error
issue_bodies:
This only happens in the beta version for 0.12.0 of Pass for iOS. In 0.11.0 cloning the same repository works fine. It seems like the update of ObjectiveGit from "0.15" to "master" in 6581f159 causes the issue. 

The error "Unsupported URL protocol" could mean that `libssh2` is not available or not properly configured as mentioned [here](https://github.com/libgit2/objective-git/issues/640#issuecomment-357687416).

candidates.body: Extends the implementation of #285 by creating a custom scale bar
issue_bodies: 1. It seems nice to have a scale bar to indicate the distance for user. MapKit comes with one for free. 2. Fixed a MapKit location status check warning https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3df513a7-9b32-4d8f-b4ba-e901a590c5b8
candidates.author: merlos

candidates.title: Features/map cache
candidates.body:
Removed "Cache" as dependency (and CryptoSwift as it was the only pod using it)
Uses "MapCache" (http://github.com/merlos/MapCache), an specialized cache for mapping. The result is a leaner build.

The cache does not have an expiration date. If user wants to get a new version of the map he shall clear the cache using Preferences.

Also, a new feature was added. User see the Cache size, which was missing in previous version, and I personally find pretty useful.

Fixes #74 and #96
issue_titles: clear cache button doesn't seem to have any effect
Map caching may fail to work unexpectedly on certain tiles
issue_bodies:
It appears that third party map remains even after clicking the clear cache button. 
Guess clear cache isn't working?

Not sure if this is related:
```
ERROR saving in cache: error(Cache.StorageError.deallocated)
ERROR saving in cache: value()
```
appears recursively in output console in Xcode

It appears to save though.

Hi, 

I open the app with internet connection and download a area. Hard close the app the turn on flight mode. Open the app and I can’t see any map tiles . 

Have I made something wrong ?
candidates.author: merlos

candidates.title: Changes for Xcode 14, iOS 16, new devices
candidates.body:
Changes:
- Minimum deployment for iOS and watchOS to comply with Xcode 14.
   - iOS 9 ~> iOS 11
   - watchOS 2 ~> watchOS 4
   - Fix deprecation notices brought by min deployment change
- Change modern `MKCompassButton` implementation added in #219, to rectify inability to use `@available` on lazy variables in Xcode 14
- Fix #232, overlay issue specific to iOS 16 only, where third party tiles will always try to be top most overlay
- Changes to MapCache, using a branch for now
- Changes to CoreGPX, using a branch for now
- Fix #233, by extensively changing top elements from fixed frame initialisations to using constraints respecting safe areas
- Rectify warnings, still 1 CoreLocation UI unresponsiveness left.
issue_titles: Map tiles are covering the track polyline on iOS 16 / Xcode 14.0
iPhone 14 Pro series safe area misalignment
issue_bodies: Uses `MKCompassButton` introduced in iOS 11, rather than repositioning the map's built in compass view, via private methods, going forwards.
This seems to be a general problem in iOS 16 / Xcode 14.0: Tiles of non-Apple maps are covering the polyline overlays all the time; so the tracks are not visible on non-Apple maps.

candidates.author: jdisho

candidates.title: big refactoring: UnifiedKey/を導入し、FlickとQwertyの実装を共通化
candidates.body:
resolve #553 
## 全体概要
このプルリクエストでは、キーボードテーマとキーのモデルに関するロジックを整理し、レイアウト依存の特殊処理を排除してコードを単純化しています。テーマアクセスを静的プロパティ化することで扱いやすくし、保守性を向上させています。

---

## 主な変更点

### テーマ処理の単純化
- QWERTYやフリックといったレイアウトに依存した色分けやハイライト処理を削除。
- `AzooKeySpecificTheme.native` と `default` を関数から静的プロパティに変更し、コード全体で利用箇所を修正。

### レイアウト依存ロジックの削除
- `Design.colors` のカラー取得処理をレイアウト非依存のプロパティに変更。
- フォント選択ロジックを修正し、レイアウト依存を削除。代わりにテキストの長さに基づいてフォントサイズを決定。

### プロトコルとモデルの一般化
- `ApplicationSpecificKeyboardViewExtensionLayoutDependentDefaultThemeProvidable` を静的プロパティベースに変更。
- `QwertyCustomKeysValue.compiled` がより汎用的な `[any UnifiedKeyModelProtocol<Extension>]` を返すように変更。
- `KeyLabelType` に `Sendable` と `Equatable` を実装し、並行処理の安全性と比較の容易さを向上。

### クリーンアップと小改善
- `LongpressActionType.none` を public に公開。
- `VariableStates` のドキュメントを改善し、バリエーション空間変化の検出に使うことを明示。

### 不要コードの削除
- 旧 `FlickKeyModelProtocol.swift` を削除し、フリックキー専用の背景スタイル処理を廃止。

---

## 効果
- テーマとキー描画の処理がレイアウトに依存しなくなり、コードの一貫性と保守性が向上。
- プロトコル・モデルの一般化により再利用性が高まり、並行処理でも安全に利用可能に。
issue_titles: refactoring: フリックのキーボードの実装とQwertyのキーボードの実装をマージする
issue_bodies:
* 現在、KeyboardViewsではFlickとQwertyで異なるキーボード実装を用いている
* これは理想的ではないのでマージし、統一した実装に整理したい
candidates.author: ensan-hcl
